text: This is Jennifer knitting a dishcloth that she started back in, uh, when?
March. Back in March, and, uh, it's pretty far along, but she had to wait till I came down here
in, uh, end of June to teach her how to finish it. But she's doing pretty good, actually. Look
how fast she's knitting. She's actually doing very wonder-mous. I'm so proud of my daughter.
I told her I needed to teach her crochet while I was here, and she says, oh, no, that's okay. I
don't want to, but I'm going to force her, and I'm going to make her. That means I'm going to
forget the knitting stitches. No, you won't. It's going to be, like, half knitting, half crocheting.
No, no, no, that doesn't happen. I promise you. Look how fast she's whipping them little fingers
again. She's definitely my daughter. She's definitely double jaunted. Oh, yeah. Yeah,
Katie. The first time Katie saw my hands, she was like, what's wrong with your hands?
